description: Paper trail of articles this subagent has shipped. Append one entry per publish.

entries.notes: Seed article. Pillar piece covering the highest-volume rental term in the tracker.
PILLAR ARTICLE. Comprehensive MUTCD guide covering device categories, barricade types, AFADs, flagger requirements, and crashworthiness standards.
Type A/B/C specs, trailer vs truck-mounted decision matrix, daily/weekly/monthly rates, lamp requirements per MUTCD §6L.06.
MUTCD §6E.04 operational rules, STOP/SLOW vs Red/Yellow Lens types, single-lane traffic requirement, purchase vs rental economics.
Type I/II/III per MUTCD §6F.63, material comparison (steel vs plastic vs water-filled), speed/duration decision tree, provider vetting checklist.
Portable signals (ATSSA-certified), rumble strips, cones vs drums, NCHRP 350 / MASH crashworthiness, portable vs permanent decision framework.

PILLAR ARTICLE. Solution-hub covering all 5 MUTCD device categories, sizing table by speed limit (MUTCD Tables 6C-1 and 6F-1), arrow board mandatory-use rules, MASH/NCHRP 350 crashworthiness, and OSHA 1926.200 compliance. No heroImage (no verified photography available).

TACTICAL ARTICLE. 28" vs. 36" MUTCD Section 6F.64 height requirements, taper and tangent spacing table (MUTCD Table 6C-2), retroreflectivity rules, rental rates, cones vs. drums decision guide. Internal links to 28-inch-traffic-cone, 36-inch-traffic-cone, channelizing-drum products and cones-drums category.

TACTICAL/HYBRID ARTICLE. Compares the five trailer-mounted device families on a typical work zone — arrow boards (MUTCD §6F.61, §6L.06), PCMS (§6F.60), AFADs (§6E.04), TMAs (NCHRP 350 / MASH TL-2/TL-3), and light towers (OSHA 1926.56). Includes function-vs-zone comparison table, MASH crashworthiness via the FHWA Office of Safety roadside hardware program, sizing rules by operation class and road speed, 2026 rental rate ranges, and combo-trailer tradeoffs. Internal links to /category/arrow-boards, /category/message-boards, and to arrow-board-rental-guide, automated-flagger-assistance-device-afad-guide, traffic-control-equipment-rental, traffic-control-rental-guide. No heroImage (no verified photography available).

PILLAR ARTICLE — highest opportunity score in the new keyword set (vol 50k, comp 1). Solution-hub pattern with 8 sections: cone zone meaning + DOT public-awareness origin, why setup matters, equipment list with inline links to 28-inch-traffic-cone, 36-inch-traffic-cone, channelizing-drum, roll-up-road-work-ahead, telescoping-sign-stand, trailer-mounted-arrow-board-15, MUTCD Part 6 + OSHA 1926.200/.201, sizing table by speed (advance warning, taper, cone spacing, count), five common mistakes, rental flow. Inline research link to FHWA MUTCD Knowledge Center. /category/cones-drums in related products. No heroImage.

PILLAR ARTICLE — head category term (vol 50k, comp 83). Solution-hub covering five barricade families: Type I, Type II, Type III, water-filled plastic, pedestrian. Includes barricade-vs-barrier distinction, MUTCD §6F.63 spec table (rails, height, use, reference), spacing/lights table by application, MASH crashworthiness via FHWA roadside hardware program, service-life expectations. Inline research link to FHWA MUTCD Knowledge Center. Internal links to type-iii-barricade, type-ii-barricade, economy-pedestrian-barricade-orange-eg, urbanite-water-filled-pedestrian-barricade, type-b-flashing-warning-light products and /category/barricades-barriers. No heroImage.

PILLAR ARTICLE — specific subtype with consumer/event intent (vol 50k, comp 96). Solution-hub: Jersey-shape origin and engineering history, plastic-vs-concrete tradeoff matrix, five common use cases (events, parking lots, low-speed work zones, highway work, security perimeters), MASH TL-2/TL-3/TL-4 by speed, run-length sizing math with end-treatment requirement, ASTM F2656 anti-vehicle distinction. Inline research link to FHWA Office of Safety roadside hardware program. Internal links to water-filled-barrier-6ft, urbanite-water-filled-pedestrian-barricade, type-iii-barricade products and /category/barricades-barriers. No heroImage.

PILLAR ARTICLE — head pillar (vol 50k, comp 61). Solution-hub covering the four MUTCD sign families (regulatory/warning/guide/signal) plus work-zone orange-warning subset. Includes family color/shape/MUTCD-Part comparison table, work-zone sign sequence (W20-1 → W21-7 → W20-5 → R2-5b), Table 6F-1 sign sizing by speed, FHWA Minimum Retroreflectivity Standards (23 CFR 655). Inline research link to FHWA MUTCD Knowledge Center. Internal links to roll-up-road-work-ahead, roll-up-flagger-ahead, roll-up-one-lane-road, telescoping-sign-stand, trailer-mounted-arrow-board-15, portable-message-board-3line products and /category/signs-sign-stands. No heroImage.

PILLAR ARTICLE — pedestrian-side device family (vol 50k, comp 94). Absorbs 'pedestrian crossing signs' (50k) and 'pedestrian crossing signal' (50k) as secondary keywords. Solution-hub covering W11-2, S1-1, W11-15, R1-6, R9-9/R9-10/R9-11, fluorescent yellow-green sheeting per §2A.11, RRFB and PHB beacon thresholds, ADA / PROWAG TPAR requirements (48-inch clear width, 5% grade, detectable edges), Type IX/XI prismatic sheeting requirement. Inline research link to FHWA MUTCD Knowledge Center. Internal links to economy-pedestrian-barricade-orange-eg, urbanite-water-filled-pedestrian-barricade, telescoping-sign-stand, galvanized-crowd-control-fence-panel-6x10 products and /category/pedestrian-control. No heroImage.
Backfilled to publish log during audit 2026-04-28. Published before log formalization.
